title: Example 3.24 Sliding-Tape Method for the Convolution Sum
summary: This page presents Example 3.24, illustrating the sliding-tape method for computing the discrete-time convolution sum of two sequences x[n] and g[n]. It explains how to reverse and shift one sequence, then multiply and add overlapping samples to obtain c[n] for positive and negative n, with resulting values and asymptotic behavior described.
